executive_summary.tertiary_root_cause: none — training is deterministic, FRED data was effectively identical for training rows

hypothesis_A_non_determinism.test: Two consecutive RecessionEnsembleModel.fit() runs on identical feature frame

hypothesis_B_fred_revisions.test: Diff indicators.csv raw-FRED columns between efb307e and 0411da9
hypothesis_B_fred_revisions.verdict: FALSIFIED — training data was effectively identical

evidence.gfc_at_risk_diffusion_change_is_artifact: stale on-disk indicators.csv persistence (Apr-23 snapshot missed B3 credit-supply columns even though Apr-23 code already generated them at runtime)
evidence.actual_training_frame_identity_verdict: effectively identical for training rows (train ends ~2025-04; only nowcast row 2026-04 has any raw-FRED change)

hypothesis_C_feature_selection_instabili.severity: MEDIUM — swap at 50-cap boundary; both features near-equally-weakly-ranked

hypothesis_D_threshold_sensitivity.verdict: CONFIRMED — this is the PRIMARY driver of the 8-month GFC lead-time swing

evidence.interpretation: F1 objective has a wide flat plateau; tie-break flips ends of the plateau on tiny input shifts
evidence.gfc_lead_mechanism: GFC probability trajectory crosses 0.30 months before it crosses 0.41; threshold flip → lead-time swings discontinuously without any underlying model change

publication_lag_correctness.training_path_detail: update_job.py:843 passes df_features from engineer_features(df_raw) without applying PUBLICATION_LAGS — training sees perfect-foresight data; only backtester applies lags

rare_event_handling.overfitting_check: WARNING — train AUC 0.99+ vs CV 0.92-0.96 shows measurable overfit; partially mitigated by ensemble calibration + gating

small_sample_stability.threshold_plateau_width_tracked: FAIL — no metric tracks plateau width today

regime_forecast_split.expanding_normalizer_era_comparability: WARNING — not a bug, but non-stationary sample size

high.title: Threshold-plateau instability drives lead-time measurement artifacts
high.impact: Lead-time metrics swing by ~3-8 months baseline-to-baseline without any underlying model change; challenger comparisons on re-tuned thresholds misattribute noise to code
high.fix: Elevate C5 Lead_Months_Fixed to primary metric; emit threshold_plateau_width in manifest

high.title: Training path does not apply publication lags
high.impact: Training sees perfect-foresight macro data while backtest honors lag — CV metrics are optimistic relative to live performance
high.fix: Apply PUBLICATION_LAGS before engineer_features in production training path

medium.title: Feature selection 1-slot churn at 50-cap boundary
medium.fix: Deterministic alphabetical tie-break on composite scores

medium.title: Large parameter search on ~600 training months
medium.fix: Optionally reduce max_features from 50 to ~30 (experimental tradeoff)

medium.title: Expanding normalizers have non-stationary sample sizes
medium.fix: Optional — rolling 30-year window for regime-sensitive features

low.title: Isotonic calibrator step-boundary contributor to threshold flip (already captured in HIGH-1)
JOLTS publication lag of 2 months may be mildly optimistic

recommended_fix_order.fix: Elevate Lead_Months_Fixed to primary lead-time metric in reports/backtests
Emit threshold_plateau_width in run_manifest.json + flag when plateau is flat
Document expected CV PR-AUC / lead-time noise floor in README

c2_resumption_note: YES — pipeline is deterministic with no critical bugs. Adopt C5 fixed-threshold gate as primary metric; Fix-1 (elevate Lead_Months_Fixed) should land before next C2 retro-comparison.
patches_written: scripts/audit_patches/fix_threshold_plateau_diagnostics.py — demonstrates threshold-plateau-width metric emission (DEMO ONLY, not applied to main)
